LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT ANDRE HAKKAK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT'S THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, AND IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IN GOOD FAITH, ADDITIONAL AND SPECIFIC FACTS TO MEET THE HEIGHTENED PLEADING STANDARD FOR A CAUSE OF ACTION OF FRAUD. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT MARISSA SHIPMAN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT'S FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC RELATIONS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SHIPMAN CANNOT INTERFERE WITH A CONTRACT IN WHICH SHE HAS A LEGITIMATE INTEREST, AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT ALLEGES SHIPMAN WAS AN AGENT. SEE CROSS-COMPLAINT, 6. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC RELATIONS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT MARISSA SHIPMAN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained as to the Cross-Complaint's fourth and fifth causes of action, and is overruled as to the sixth cause of action. As to the fourth cause of action for intentional interference with economic relations, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Shipman cannot interfere with a contract in which she has a legitimate interest, and Cross-Complainant alleges Shipman was an agent. See Cross-Complaint, 6. As to the fifth cause of action for negligent interference with economic relations, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT ANDRE HAKKAK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained as to the Cross-Complaint's third cause of action, and is overruled as to the second and sixth causes of action. As to the third cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to allege, in good faith, additional and specific facts to meet the heightened pleading standard for a cause of action of fraud. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185
=(302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANTS ANDRE HAKKAK & MARISSA SHIPMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE request for Punitive Damages and Attorney's fee in the CROSS COMPLAINT - The motion to strike as to paragraphs 27 and 32 is moot, in light of the ruling on the demurrers. The motion to strike line 5 of the prayer for relief is granted. There is no basis for punitive damages. The motion to strike the request for attorney fees is granted. There is no basis for attorneys fees pled in the Cross-Complaint. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
